name: Debtors Anonymous
description_short: Debtors Anonymous is a free 12-step fellowship for people struggling with compulsive debting, offering peer-led meetings across the Greater New York area.
description_long: Debtors Anonymous of Greater New York is a peer-support fellowship based on 12-step principles, designed specifically for people whose problematic relationship with unsecured debt is causing significant life disruption. The organization has been operating in the Greater New York region and uses a community-driven model rather than professional counseling or debt management services. The program offers multiple meeting formats including in-person gatherings, telephone meetings, and virtual sessions held across Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, Long Island, Westchester, Connecticut, and Upstate New York locations. Members access tools including the 12 Steps, Traditions, Promises, and spiritual principles to transform their relationship with money and debt. What distinguishes Debtors Anonymous is its completely free, volunteer-led structure with no professional staff, fees, or commercial debt resolution services. The organization relies on member contributions and emphasizes mutual support among people with shared experiences of compulsive debting. Members are encouraged to attend at least six meetings to determine if the program fits their needs. The organization provides a self-assessment tool with 15 questions to help potential members evaluate whether they qualify as compulsive debtors. This approach is best suited for people who identify as having a behavioral or psychological relationship problem with debt rather than those seeking professional debt consolidation or credit repair. A significant caveat is that this is peer support, not professional financial counseling, and members may need additional resources for concrete debt restructuring, legal issues, or bankruptcy considerations.
address: One Union Square South, 853 Broadway, New York, NY 10003

company_info.headquarters: One Union Square South, 853 Broadway, New York, NY 10003

pros: Completely free peer-led fellowship with no fees or memberships costs
Multiple meeting formats available (in-person, phone, and virtual) across seven geographic areas
12-step spiritual framework designed specifically for compulsive debting behavior
Encourages at least six meeting attendance before commitment, allowing trial participation
Provides self-assessment tool (15-question screening) to help identify if program is right for you
Community-driven with volunteer leadership, no commercial debt settlement involvement
Meetings available seven days per week across different times and locations
cons: Peer support model with no professional financial advisors or certified credit counselors on staff
Not a debt consolidation or settlement service—does not negotiate with creditors or restructure debt
Limited to Greater New York geographic area; services primarily regional rather than national
Requires ongoing meeting attendance and commitment to 12-step principles; not a short-term fix
No formal financial literacy or budgeting instruction provided through the program
best_for: People who identify compulsive or behavioral spending and debting patterns requiring psychological/spiritual intervention
Individuals seeking free peer support and community connection around debt and money issues
Those in the Greater New York region looking for 12-step fellowship similar to AA or NA but focused on debt
People combining DA participation with professional credit counseling or debt management services elsewhere
diagnosis: Debtors Anonymous is best for people in the Greater New York area who recognize a compulsive or behavioral pattern in their spending and debt accumulation and seek free peer support through 12-step principles. However, members should be aware this is not a professional debt management, credit counseling, or financial restructuring service—those seeking to negotiate with creditors, consolidate debt, or repair credit should combine DA participation with services from other providers.
services: In-person peer support meetings held across Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, Long Island, Westchester, Connecticut, and Upstate New York
Virtual/internet-based meetings via Zoom for remote participation
Telephone-based meetings for those unable to attend in person
12-step framework and spiritual principles tailored to compulsive debting
Self-assessment questionnaire (15-question tool) to evaluate program fit
Program tools and resources including Steps, Traditions, and Promises
Fellowship community and mutual support with others struggling with compulsive debt
Weekly meetings scheduled across all seven days of the week at varying times
Volunteer-led sponsorship and mentoring relationships within fellowship
Fellowship Festival and community fundraising events
Digital payment options (PayPal) for voluntary member contributions
